data.title: Article highlights importance of early guidance for franchise success. Key factors for thriving franchisees shared. From Shohola, Pa. Published on Sept. 18, 2025.
data.description: An article discusses the importance of early guidance, validation, and community for franchise owners. According to Emily Romero, based in Shohola, Pennsylvania, dated September 18, 2025, this support can significantly impact the success of franchise operators. The article emphasizes that having access to such resources from the beginning can be crucial, with research showing that operators who receive early-stage guidance are more likely to thrive compared to those who do not. This insight underscores the hidden value in seeking support early on in the journey of franchise ownership.

data.title: PENGU surges by double digits as forecasters predict further growth, despite flat trading of Pudgy Penguins NFTs.
data.description: According to prediction market users, there is a strong belief that PENGU will experience continued growth, while the trading activity of the Pudgy Penguins NFT collection has remained relatively stable.

data.title: Blackline Safety lands $1.8M contract with Californian water utility for 560+ devices and 4 years of services.
data.description: In Calgary, Canada, Blackline Safety Corp., a prominent player in connected safety technology (TSX: BLN), has disclosed a significant development. The company secured a fresh $1.8 million contract with a major water and wastewater utility provider in North America, headquartered in California. The deal encompasses over 560 devices, consisting of G7c wearable gas detectors and Blackline Docks to facilitate device calibration and charging processes. Additionally, the contract involves provision for four years of self-monitoring services to maintain continuous visibility and safety assurance.

data.title: Håkan Buskhe named Chair of SKF Automotive Board, part of AB SKF. Announcement made by SKF Board of Directors.
data.description: The Board of Directors in Gothenburg, Sweden, has revealed their plan to name Håkan Buskhe as the future Chair of the SKF Automotive division. This business segment remains under AB SKF's umbrella. This development was made public on September 18, 2025.

data.title: Sverica Capital Management sells Med First, a healthcare company, after growing it from 13 to 27 clinics, quadrupling profitability.
data.description: Sverica Capital Management LP shared today the successful sale of RMS Healthcare Management, also known as "Med First." Under Sverica's ownership, Med First expanded its clinics from 13 to 27, with two more in the pipeline, and boosted profitability by almost fourfold. This growth elevated its status as a key player in North Carolina's primary care sector. Med First, operating since 2007, is based in Raleigh, NC.

data.title: Trially AI raises $4.7M in Seed Round, unveils "Margo" to address clinical trial recruitment obstacle.
data.description: In Kansas City, Missouri, Trially®, an AI-focused platform for clinical trial recruitment, has revealed that it successfully raised $4.7 million in funding. The company has also introduced Margo, an innovative AI system designed to enhance trial enrollment rates by transforming potential patient matches into active participants. Currently, clinical trial recruitment stands as a pressing issue in healthcare, with a significant 86% of trials experiencing delays due to recruitment issues. This setback has led to the pharmaceutical sector enduring daily losses exceeding $600,000. Through the utilization of Margo, Trially® aims to address these challenges and revolutionize the clinical trial recruitment process.

data.title: Families sue Boeing and Honeywell for catastrophic Air India crash, alleging a defective fuel cutoff switch led to 242 deaths.
data.description: Boeing Co. (NYSE: BA) is currently facing legal challenges as families of victims from a fatal crash in India have filed a lawsuit against the company along with Honeywell International Inc. (NASDAQ: HON). The lawsuit, filed in Delaware Superior Court, involves four victims from the Air India Flight 171 crash, which resulted in the deaths of 242 individuals onboard and 19 others on the ground. Allegations suggest that a faulty fuel cutoff switch led to the tragic incident in June. The lawsuit claims that based on flight data, a pilot unintentionally disabled fuel to the engines of the Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner, resulting in a total loss of thrust shortly after takeoff. Furthermore, Boeing is set to deliver its first 737 Max aircraft to VietJet, nine years after the initial order was placed.

data.title: Schneider Electric unveils new AI infrastructure reference designs, supporting NVIDIA Mission Control and GB300 NVL72, targeting AI factories.
data.description: A groundbreaking AI infrastructure reference design incorporating controls has been introduced for optimal OT/IT interoperability with NVIDIA Mission Control and enterprise applications. This innovation, in collaboration with NVIDIA, features the cutting-edge NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 power and cooling reference design, essential for the rollout of future Blackwell GPUs. These dual reference designs empower data center operators to enhance the implementation speed of AI infrastructure for AI manufacturing facilities located anywhere.

data.title: Mill Creek starts preleasing at Modera Westside Trail in Atlanta, bringing 373 homes to West Midtown.
data.description: A new midrise community is set to add 373 homes to Atlanta's West Midtown neighborhood. The project, announced by Mill Creek Residential on September 18, 2025, marks a significant addition to the area. Mill Creek Residential is renowned for its focus on high-quality rental properties nationwide. This development is poised to provide a substantial housing boost to the Atlanta neighborhood, catering to the growing demand for modern living spaces.

data.title: XTM sells assets to Pateno Payments for $3 million, boosting fintech capabilities. #BusinessWire #Fintech
data.description: In Toronto, fintech company XTM Inc. (QB: XTMIF / CSE: PAID / FSE: 7XT) has completed the sale of its assets to Pateno Payments Inc., a Digital Commerce Group subsidiary, for a total of USD $3,000,000. The deal, originally announced on April 29, 2025, was finalized on September. XTM is known for its innovative services in automated tip pooling, instant payouts, and Earned Wage Access ("EWA") via its AnyDay™ platform.
